signals.reasoning: BTC is still below the recent breakdown area, but the tape has shifted into sideways stabilization rather than fresh downside expansion. With the active geopolitical veto still in force, this is not a clean new trade.

ETH still leans weak on structure and has fresh-shorts-pressing characteristics, but the actual chart is now chopping after the dump instead of breaking lower cleanly. The setup does not clear the bar for a real fresh signal under veto.
SOL has some squeeze fuel from negative funding, but the price action is just a modest bounce inside a damaged range. Not enough edge to force a long or short here.

0G is the only real squeeze-long candidate on the board because funding is extremely negative and price is still pressing higher. I am keeping confidence capped because the move is already extended, liquidity is not ideal, and the event veto is still active.
GOAT remains too washed out and too squeeze-prone to trust. The negative-funding fuel is real, but the chart damage and liquidity risk make this watch-only.

HEMI is still one of the weaker charts and funding remains elevated enough to keep a bearish lean alive, but it has already flushed hard and the squeeze risk is growing. This is only a reduced-confidence fade under veto.
BRETT is showing a short-covering rally rather than a clean directional setup. That makes it a poor candidate for either side right now.
DYM is still weak, but the move already looks like an exhausted liquidation phase rather than a fresh continuation trigger. Watch only.

DASH remains one of the cleaner bearish names because the broader structure is still broken and the board still reads fresh shorts pressing rather than a full squeeze. Confidence stays reduced because price is already off the lows and veto is active.
GRIFFAIN is damaged but not breaking lower cleanly now, and the OI-to-volume ratio is too messy to trust. Better to leave it alone.
AVNT has squeeze fuel from negative funding, but the bounce is weak and the chart is still unstable. It does not clear as a real long under current macro conditions.
2Z is trying to base after the drop, but the edge is still too thin and too crowded to trust. Watchlist only.
SPX has already flushed hard and is now stabilizing rather than trending. The easy part of the short is gone, and there is not enough long edge to flip sides.
TNSR is still weak, but the liquidity is poor and the move is already mature. This is not a strong enough continuation short to promote.
NOT still has a bearish mechanical profile, but it is far too thin and noisy to recommend under a live veto. The board score overstates the real tradeability.
MAV is bouncing after the dump, which makes new shorts late and new longs premature. Not actionable.
ACE remains weak on the sheet, but the market depth is poor and the chart is already in post-flush territory. It stays watch-only.

FARTCOIN is still one of the cleaner bearish continuations because price is weak again and open interest is no longer collapsing with it, which keeps the fresh-shorts-pressing thesis alive. Confidence is reduced because the event veto is still active and this name can whipsaw hard.
BLUR is weak but already in a developed move, and the current tape is more stabilization than breakdown. Not enough edge for a fresh position.

STBL still deserves a reduced-confidence bearish lean because funding is the most crowded positive on the board and the structure remains damaged. I am capping conviction because it has already flushed a lot and squeeze risk is not trivial.
